page_content:
Formerly: FILM329.

CINE331 Kafka and Film: The Uncanny in Literature and Film (3 Credits) Analysis of major works by Franz Kafka (1883-1924), his affinity to the cinema and use of cinematic means and techniques (e.g. the gaze, flashback, parallel action, gesture and body language, etc.) in his writings, as well as examination of adaptations of Kafka narratives (e.g. the Orson Welles and David Jones adaptations of The Trial, 1961, 1992) and other films that use Kafkaian themes (e.g. Steven Soderbergh’s ‘Kafka’, 1991). Cross-listed with: GERS331.

Credit Only Granted for. GERM331, GERS331, or CINE331.
